turns.user_message: I've finally accepted that I am just naturally wired to brace for impact. It feels like my internal alarm system never really powers down, even when everything around me is completely fine. I spend so much energy trying to predict every little thing that could go sideways, and it leaves me completely drained by the afternoon. I know it sounds exhausting, but I just can't seem to flip the switch and actually relax. It's like I'm constantly waiting for a storm that never actually shows up.

This constant state of readiness has been my reality for so long now that it feels completely normal. My chest gets tight at the slightest change in tone or unexpected delay, and I immediately start mapping out disaster scenarios. I try to tell myself it is just a temporary glitch, but the tension just builds and builds until I am physically shaking. It drains all the joy out of my day because I am too busy preparing for things that will probably never happen. I just want to feel safe in my own head for once.

The anticipation has completely taken over my entire nervous system and I cannot catch my breath no matter how hard I try. Every single quiet moment feels like a trap waiting to snap shut, so I keep myself constantly busy just to outrun the dread. My hands tremble and my thoughts spiral so fast that I can barely remember what a calm afternoon even feels like. It is exhausting to live like this, always bracing for a collapse that never comes but always feels inevitable. I am just so tired of fighting my own brain every single day.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This has been going on for a while.
